document.title: PHI Security Risk Register — HIPAA-Compliant Telehealth Platform
document.methodology: STRIDE + FMEA hybrid. RPN = Severity × Likelihood × (6 - Detectability). Scales: Severity 1-5, Likelihood 1-5, Detectability 1-5 (5=easily detected).
document.review_cycle: Annual or after material security event

risk_register.title: Patient Identity Spoofing via Credential Stuffing
risk_register.threat_description: Attacker uses breached credentials to authenticate as a patient, gaining full PHI access including medical history, prescriptions, and appointment records.
risk_register.vulnerability: Password reuse by patients, absent or bypassable MFA
risk_register.existing_controls: Password complexity requirements, basic rate limiting
risk_register.severity_rationale: Full PHI access for compromised account; HIPAA breach notification required
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Credential stuffing is commodity attack; healthcare sector targeted frequently
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Login anomalies partially detectable via geo/velocity; credential stuffing may blend with normal traffic

required_controls.description: Mandatory MFA for all patient accounts
Rate limiting ≤5 attempts/min/IP + CAPTCHA on login
Breached password check via HaveIBeenPwned API
Geo-velocity anomaly detection → step-up auth

risk_register.title: Provider Identity Spoofing for Fraudulent Prescription
risk_register.threat_description: Attacker compromises provider credentials via phishing and issues fraudulent controlled substance prescriptions using the provider's DEA registration.
risk_register.vulnerability: Provider credential phishing, absent hardware MFA for prescription actions
risk_register.severity_rationale: Patient harm from controlled substance misuse; DEA regulatory action; HIPAA breach
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Providers are high-value phishing targets; SMS MFA bypassable via SIM swap
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Anomalous prescribing volume may be detected; single fraudulent Rx difficult to catch in real time

required_controls.description: FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware MFA for all prescription actions
Re-authentication required after 15 min idle before prescribing
ML-based prescribing anomaly detection (volume, drug class, geography)

risk_register.title: Tenant Isolation Bypass via Header Manipulation
phi_categories_at_risk: All 18 HIPAA identifiers for target tenant's entire patient population
risk_register.threat_description: Attacker manipulates X-SAGE-Tenant header to access another tenant's PHI schema, bypassing multi-tenant isolation.
risk_register.vulnerability: Tenant validation tied only to header value, not cryptographically bound to JWT
risk_register.severity_rationale: Mass PHI breach — entire tenant population. HIPAA breach notification for all affected individuals.
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Requires valid authentication + knowledge of target tenant ID
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Cross-tenant access may appear as normal activity without specific monitoring

required_controls.description: Bind tenant ID to JWT claims — header must match signed token
PostgreSQL Row-Level Security as defense-in-depth

risk_register.title: E-Prescription Tampering — Drug/Dosage Modification
risk_register.threat_description: Attacker with write access modifies prescription content (drug name, dosage, patient identity) after provider signature but before pharmacy transmission.
risk_register.vulnerability: Prescription records mutable without cryptographic integrity protection
risk_register.threat_actor: Drug diverter, malicious insider, external attacker
risk_register.severity_rationale: Patient harm from wrong medication or dosage is life-threatening; DEA violation
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Requires privileged database access or API access control bypass
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Modification detectable by comparing to provider's original signed version if signing implemented

required_controls.description: Cryptographic prescription signing (ECDSA P-384) by prescribing provider
Immutable prescription audit trail with hash chain
Pharmacy-side signature verification before dispensing

risk_register.title: Audit Log Tampering to Conceal PHI Breach
phi_categories_at_risk: Audit metadata — not direct PHI but destroys breach detection capability
risk_register.threat_description: Attacker with database access deletes or modifies audit log entries to erase evidence of unauthorized PHI access, defeating HIPAA compliance requirements.
risk_register.vulnerability: Mutable audit log database with DBA access grants
risk_register.severity_rationale: Destroys HIPAA §164.312(b) compliance; masks breach from detection and notification
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Requires DBA-level access; insider threat scenario
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Deletion of audit records removes the evidence needed to detect the tampering

required_controls.description: WORM storage for audit logs (S3 Object Lock, compliance mode)
Hash chain integrity — each log entry references SHA-256 of prior
Real-time streaming to immutable external SIEM (no local delete path)

risk_register.title: AI Symptom Checker Adversarial Input — Triage Manipulation
risk_register.threat_description: Adversarially crafted inputs manipulate AI triage model to return dangerously incorrect recommendations, potentially downgrading urgent conditions to low priority.
risk_register.vulnerability: No input validation layer before model inference; model not tested against adversarial inputs
risk_register.threat_actor: Sophisticated attacker, disgruntled patient, security researcher
risk_register.severity_rationale: Delayed emergency care from incorrect triage is potentially fatal
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Requires AI adversarial expertise; healthcare models less commonly targeted than financial
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Adversarial inputs may not trigger obvious anomalies; model output may look plausible

required_controls.description: Semantic input validation layer before model inference
Low-confidence outputs escalate to human triage automatically
Emergency keyword bypass — chest pain, stroke symptoms always → urgent pathway

risk_register.risk_owner: Chief Medical Officer + ML Platform Lead

risk_register.title: FHIR Bulk Export — Mass PHI Exfiltration
phi_categories_at_risk: All 18 HIPAA identifiers for entire tenant patient population
risk_register.threat_description: Compromised integration partner uses FHIR $export operation to download entire patient population PHI for a tenant within minutes.
risk_register.vulnerability: FHIR $export accessible to any system-level OAuth client without additional approval gate
risk_register.threat_actor: Compromised integration partner, malicious insider, nation-state
risk_register.severity_rationale: Mass PHI breach — potentially millions of patient records; mandatory notification for all affected
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Integration partner compromise is a realistic attack vector; supply chain incidents increasing
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Large data transfer may trigger volume-based alerts; pattern recognizable in access logs

required_controls.description: FHIR $export requires explicit tenant admin approval — HITL gate
Rate limit bulk export: max 1000 records/min; alert on full export
DLP: PHI export destinations restricted to systems with active BAAs
Bulk export anomaly alert → SOC immediate investigation

risk_register.title: PHI Leakage via Mobile Push Notifications on Lock Screen
risk_register.threat_description: Push notifications containing PHI displayed on device lock screen, visible to anyone holding the device without authentication.
risk_register.vulnerability: Notification payload includes PHI content; no lock-screen suppression policy
risk_register.severity_rationale: Incidental PHI disclosure; HIPAA violation; patient privacy harm
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Extremely common — most notifications include content by default; device sharing is frequent
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Physical disclosure events are invisible to platform monitoring

required_controls.description: Push notifications contain ZERO PHI — message only: 'You have a new message'
App requires biometric/PIN re-auth before displaying notification content
Server-side enforcement of notification content policy — cannot be overridden by client

risk_register.title: S3 PHI Bucket Public Misconfiguration
phi_categories_at_risk: All uploaded PHI files for misconfigured tenant
risk_register.threat_description: S3 bucket ACL or policy accidentally set to public, exposing all PHI objects stored in the tenant's prefix to the public internet.
risk_register.vulnerability: Manual S3 configuration without automated policy enforcement or detection
risk_register.severity_rationale: Mass PHI public exposure; OCR investigation; multi-million dollar penalties
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Cloud misconfiguration is extremely common; major healthcare breaches attributed to S3 misconfigs
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Without automated scanning, public buckets may persist undetected for days or weeks

required_controls.description: AWS S3 Block Public Access at organization level — cannot be overridden
IaC (Terraform) enforces private ACL — PR CI gate blocks public bucket configs
AWS Config rule alerts on public bucket within 60 seconds

risk_register.title: IDOR — Unauthorized Patient Record Access via Predictable IDs
risk_register.attack_surface: AS-04, AS-06 — EHR and Document APIs
risk_register.threat_description: Authenticated patient increments medical record ID parameter to access another patient's records without authorization check on record ownership.
risk_register.vulnerability: Sequential or predictable resource IDs without per-resource authorization check
risk_register.severity_rationale: Unauthorized PHI access for potentially thousands of patients
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: BOLA/IDOR is OWASP API Security #1; trivial to exploit if IDs are sequential
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Enumeration pattern detectable via anomaly detection; individual accesses may not trigger alerts

required_controls.description: All resource IDs are UUIDs v4 — cryptographically non-enumerable
Authorization check on every resource access — requester must own resource or have explicit grant
Automated BOLA test suite in CI pipeline

risk_register.title: DDoS Attack on Video Consultation Infrastructure
phi_categories_at_risk: N/A — availability threat with patient safety implications
risk_register.threat_description: Volumetric DDoS targeting WebRTC signaling disrupts active clinical consultations. Patients mid-consultation lose connection, potentially delaying acute care.
risk_register.vulnerability: WebRTC signaling service exposed to public internet for client connectivity
risk_register.severity_rationale: Disrupted clinical care; potential patient harm for time-sensitive consultations
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Healthcare is a top DDoS target; video infrastructure is attractive high-impact target
risk_register.detectability_rationale: DDoS traffic volume detectable; application-layer attacks harder to distinguish from legitimate traffic

required_controls.description: AWS Shield Advanced / Cloudflare with automatic DDoS mitigation
Geographic rate limiting and IP reputation filtering at CDN edge
Automatic consultation reconnect with state preservation (max 60s grace)
Automatic phone consultation fallback if video fails for >60s

risk_register.title: Ransomware Targeting PHI Storage
phi_categories_at_risk: All stored PHI — entire platform if backups inaccessible
risk_register.threat_description: Ransomware encrypts or deletes PHI databases and S3 objects. Double extortion: data exfiltrated before encryption. Without immutable backups, patient records irrecoverable.
risk_register.vulnerability: Mutable backup storage; broad lateral movement possible if network not segmented
risk_register.severity_rationale: Care disruption; mass PHI breach; HIPAA notification for all patients; financial extortion
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Healthcare is #1 ransomware target by sector; average ransom demand $1.3M
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Ransomware deployment often detected only after encryption begins; dwell time average 16 days

required_controls.description: S3 Object Lock (compliance mode, WORM) — ransomware cannot delete
3-2-1 backup strategy with offsite immutable copy in separate AWS account
Network segmentation — data tier not reachable except via application API
RTO ≤ 4h, RPO ≤ 1h — tested quarterly in DR exercise

risk_register.title: Mass Assignment Vulnerability — Self-Privilege Escalation
risk_register.attack_surface: AS-07, AS-03 — Admin and Prescription APIs
phi_categories_at_risk: All PHI accessible at elevated privilege level
risk_register.threat_description: Authenticated user submits JSON payload with 'role':'admin' or 'tenant_scope':'all' fields. Unvalidated ORM binding maps these directly to database record, granting elevated privileges.
risk_register.vulnerability: ORM mass assignment — request body fields not allowlisted; arbitrary JSON bound to model
risk_register.threat_actor: Any authenticated user, bug bounty researcher
risk_register.existing_controls: None — vulnerability by design in many ORM frameworks
risk_register.severity_rationale: Self-escalation to admin enables unrestricted platform-wide PHI access
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: OWASP API Security #6; exploitable by any authenticated user with basic API knowledge
risk_register.detectability_rationale: Role change via update endpoint may not trigger privilege escalation alerts

required_controls.description: Allowlist-based request schema validation — never bind arbitrary JSON to ORM model
Role changes require separate privileged endpoint with elevated MFA auth
SAST rules for ORM mass assignment patterns in CI pipeline

risk_register.title: SQL Injection — Tenant Isolation Bypass
risk_register.attack_surface: AS-04, AS-03, AS-08 — Multiple API Endpoints
phi_categories_at_risk: Cross-tenant PHI — potentially all tenants' patient data
risk_register.threat_description: Unsanitized filter parameters injected into SQL queries bypass schema-per-tenant isolation using UNION attacks or postgres system table access.
risk_register.vulnerability: String concatenation in SQL query construction; no parameterized queries
risk_register.threat_actor: Skilled external attacker, malicious tenant user
risk_register.existing_controls: Basic input validation on some endpoints
risk_register.severity_rationale: Multi-tenant PHI breach — complete isolation failure affects all tenants
risk_register.likelihood_rationale: Parameterized queries are standard practice; SQLi possible only where not implemented
risk_register.detectability_rationale: SQL error patterns and UNION keywords detectable by WAF; blind SQLi less visible

required_controls.description: Parameterized queries / ORM exclusively — no string concatenation in SQL
PostgreSQL RLS — app user cannot query schemas outside their tenant
WAF SQL injection ruleset (OWASP ModSecurity CRS)
Automated SQLi DAST scan in CI (every PR touching database queries)

controls: Mandatory MFA for all users (patients: TOTP; providers: FIDO2)
JWT algorithm whitelist (RS256 only, reject alg:none)
Parameterized queries everywhere — eliminate SQL injection surface
S3 Block Public Access at organization level
Audit log WORM storage (S3 Object Lock compliance mode)
DTLS-SRTP certificate pinning for video streams

compliance_gaps.gap: DEA EPCS cryptographic prescription signing not yet implemented
HIPAA §164.312(b) audit controls — audit log not yet immutable/WORM
Mobile push notification PHI policy not enforced

owasp_threats_addressed: OWASP Top 10 A01:2021 — Broken Access Control (IDOR, BOLA)
OWASP Top 10 A07:2021 — Identification and Authentication Failures
OWASP API Security API3:2023 — Broken Object Property Level Authorization
OWASP API Security API4:2023 — Unrestricted Resource Consumption
OWASP API Security API6:2023 — Mass Assignment
